name: Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail from Camarena de la Sierra
name_original: Sendero equipado Amanaderos – circular desde Camarena de la Sierra
description: Hike the difficult 14.6-mile Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail from Camarena de la Sierra, featuring stunning cascades and forests.
region_intro: Hiking around Camarena De La Sierra offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Javalambre, featuring altitudes from 1,000 to over 2,000 meters. The region is characterized by extensive pine and savin forests, numerous springs feeding the Camarena River, and rugged mountain terrain. Natural features include the Amanaderos Waterfalls and several monumental trees, contributing to a rich botanical environment. Best hiking trails around Camarena De La Sierra The most popular hiking route is Javalambre – circular from Javalambre Station via Rabadá y Navarro Refuge, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes significant elevation gain towards the Javalambre peak. Another top favourite among local hikers is Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail from Camarena de la Sierra, a difficult 14.6 miles (23.4 km) path. This trail features stunning cascades and dense forests, following part of the GR 10 long-distance route. Local hikers also love the Vicente Pine – Collado del Buey Shelter loop from Parque Natural de la Puebla de San Miguel, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through varied forest and mountain landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Camarena De La Sierra is defined by the high-altitude Sierra de Javalambre, lush forests, and numerous natural springs and waterfalls. The network of routes offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to challenging ascents. The routes in Camarena De La Sierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 8 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Camarena De La Sierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: There are over 20 hiking trails around Camarena De La Sierra, offering a diverse range of experiences. The komoot community has explored these routes more than 100 times, highlighting the area's popularity among outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: The trails in Camarena De La Sierra cater to various fitness levels. You'll find 3 easy routes, 12 moderate routes, and 5 difficult routes, ensuring there's a suitable option whether you're looking for a leisurely stroll or a challenging ascent.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: Yes, the region is known for its water features. The stunning Amanaderos Waterfalls are a significant natural attraction. You can experience them on the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974625544/amanaderos-waterfalls-and-gr-10-trail-from-camarena-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail from Camarena de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, which also follows part of the long-distance GR 10 route.

Q: What natural landmarks or points of interest can I explore while hiking?
A: Camarena de la Sierra offers numerous natural landmarks. Hikers can reach the summit of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt; for spectacular panoramic views. The area is also rich in monumental trees and is known as the 'Town of a Thousand Fountains' due to its many springs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Buey Shelter&lt;/a&gt; is another notable point of interest.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Camarena De La Sierra, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Hikers frequently praise the diverse and rugged landscapes, the extensive pine and savin forests, and the rewarding views from high-altitude points like Pico Javalambre.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976332845/javalambre-circular-from-javalambre-station-via-rabada-y-navarro-refuge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Javalambre – circular from Javalambre Station via Rabadá y Navarro Refuge&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.7-mile (9.2 km) trail. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Buey Shelter – Cerro Calderón loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.6 miles (7.5 km) long.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, there are easier options suitable for families. Look for trails classified as 'easy' in the komoot guide. These often feature less strenuous climbs and shorter distances, allowing families to enjoy the beautiful forests and natural springs at a comfortable pace.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: Generally, hiking with dogs is permitted on most trails in Camarena De La Sierra. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific routes or nature reserves.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trails in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: The terrain in Camarena De La Sierra is diverse, ranging from lush forests of pines and savins to rugged mountain paths. You'll encounter varied surfaces, including forest tracks, rocky sections, and paths along riverbeds, reflecting the region's position within the Sierra de Javalambre.

Q: Are there any refuges or shelters along the trails?
A: Yes, the area provides options for rest and shelter. The Rabadá y Navarro Refuge is located high in the Javalambre mountain range, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado del Buey Shelter&lt;/a&gt; is another notable point, offering a place to pause during your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Camarena De La Sierra?
A: Camarena De La Sierra is a superb destination for hiking year-round. Each season offers a unique experience, from the vibrant greenery of spring and summer to the rich colors of autumn and the serene, snow-dusted landscapes of winter, particularly at higher altitudes.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are 5 difficult routes available. These often involve significant elevation gain and longer distances, such as the demanding path to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3925573" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Javalambre&lt;/a&gt;, offering rewarding views from the 2,020-meter summit.
tips: Beautiful and picturesque town in the interior of Teruel that is usually the place chosen to start several hiking trails in the area, including those that ascend to the Javalambre and the one that runs through the Amanederos del Riodeva.

Ravine recently equipped with steps, staples and safety cables to be able to traverse it without technical means, previously it was just a canyoning activity to be carried out with ropes and appropriate equipment, now it can be carried out by all those who do not have vertigo and feel comfortable walking on slopes strong and steps equipped. very nice and fun

Its name refers to the surge from where the Riodeva is born. Not only is the landscape and environment of the Amanaderos something unique, but so is the path to get here. From the town of Riodeva, follow a path that in sections coincides with the GR 10 and runs along the river ravine between steps, boulevards and footbridges. A route that also runs through several waterfalls depending on the flow of the river depending on the rains.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as difficult due to its length and significant elevation gain. While the equipped section of the Amanaderos is considered accessible and suitable for families, the full route from Camarena de la Sierra covers over 23 km with nearly 1000 meters of ascent, making it a challenging hike.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for the full 23 km route is around 7 hours and 29 minutes of moving time. However, allow for additional time to enjoy the waterfalls and scenery, making it a full-day adventure.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll experience varied terrain, including dense pine forests in higher areas and Mediterranean scrub with plants like thyme and rosemary downstream. The trail follows the River Eva through a ravine, featuring numerous waterfalls and cascades. Expect sections with stairs, railings, and equipped paths to navigate the more challenging parts. The area is also geologically interesting, with dolomite and sandstone formations.

faqs.question: What notable waterfalls can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is famous for its spectacular waterfalls. You'll encounter several, including Salto de la Yegua (22m), Cascada de las Ninfas (24m), Salto Polaina (22m), and the impressive Salto de la Virgen Blanca (or San Lorenzo) with a 48-meter drop. Other notable cascades include Pedro Pérez jump (20m) and Montereta waterfall (10m). You'll also pass by The Amanaderos of Riodeva.

faqs.question: Is the Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in this region. However, given the equipped sections with cables, stairs, and railings, some parts of the trail might be challenging for dogs, especially larger ones or those not accustomed to such terrain. Always keep your dog on a leash and ensure they are comfortable with heights and varied surfaces.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is especially beautiful in spring when the vegetation is lush and the waterfalls are at their fullest due to snowmelt and rainfall. Autumn also offers pleasant temperatures and colorful foliage. Summers can be hot, so an early start is recommended, while winter might present icy conditions on some sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Amanaderos Waterfalls and GR 10 Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Camarena de la Sierra. You should be able to find parking within the village or at designated trailheads nearby. It's always a good idea to arrive early, especially on weekends or during peak season, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike in the Sierra de Javalambre?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or to pay an entrance fee to hike this trail or generally within the Sierra de Javalambre. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice Leave No Trace principles to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the GR 10, a long-distance path. Specifically, it covers significant sections of the 'De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa Camarena de la Sierra - Riodeva)' and 'De Valencia a Lisboa (Etapa Arcos de las Salinas - Camarena de la Sierra)' trails. You'll also find smaller portions intersecting with 'Barranco de los Ramos' and the 'Sendero Equipado "Amanaderos"'.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities or places to eat near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Camarena de la Sierra, where the trail begins, is a village that may offer basic amenities such as cafes or small shops. For more options, consider nearby larger towns. It's always recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your hike.

faqs.question: What makes the Amanaderos Waterfalls section particularly notable?
faqs.answer: The Amanaderos Equipped Trail section is notable for its series of spectacular waterfalls and the unique experience of navigating the ravine via equipped paths with cables and railings. It's a highlight of the GR 10 in this area, offering a blend of natural beauty and a mild adventure, making it a popular and memorable part of the hike.
